title: Johnson & Johnson — White Space
html:
Module 7: J&J White-Space BD Map

Last Updated: April 10, 2026 Purpose: Identify, prioritize, and action-plan every unsold ZoomRx service opportunity across J&J's brand portfolio. Built for use in BD conversations, AO planning, and pipeline reviews.

Section 1: Full Brand x Service Matrix

How to Read This Matrix

  • ✅ Active — currently running or recently delivered
  • 🎯 HIGH priority white space — strong fit, natural extension, client likely receptive
  • 🔵 MEDIUM priority white space — good fit but needs introduction, pilot, or champion building
  • ⚪ LOW priority white space — possible but not a natural fit right now
  • ➖ Not applicable — service does not fit this brand/TA

Lung Oncology: RYBREVANT (amivantamab) & LAZCLUZE (lazertinib)

Service RYBREVANT LAZCLUZE
PET / SFEA ✅ 🎯 Bundled with RYB but expansion into HNSCC/GI indications is a separate sell
OCE Tracking ✅ ⚪ Only relevant if separate OCE team deployed
MSL / MEE ✅ ✅ (covered under Lung MEE)
FRM Tracking ✅ ⚪ Covered under Lung FRM umbrella
KAM Tracking ✅ ⚪ Covered under Onc KAM umbrella
ATU 🎯 HIGH — 1L EGFR launch ATU is a natural ask as MARIPOSA data matures 🔵 Combo ATU with RYB more likely than standalone
HCP-Patient Conversations 🔵 SubQ CRL creates access/admin burden worth capturing ➖
Digital Tracking 🔵 Online narrative shaping is already tracked via Social Listening — digital promo tracking is the next layer ⚪
Patient Experience 🔵 Infusion-related reactions and SubQ transition create a patient experience story ⚪
Qual / Qual Research 🔵 Qual on HNSCC/GI expansion perceptions ⚪
Message Testing 🎯 HIGH — MARIPOSA OS data, SubQ launch, HNSCC expansion all need message testing ⚪
Commercial Analytics 🔵 SFTO-style Rx integration (proven with ERLEADA, DARZALEX) ⚪
Sales Simulation ⚪ ➖
Launch KPI Tracking 🎯 HIGH — HNSCC expansion launch; SubQ formulation launch when approved ➖
Ferma Congress ✅ ✅ (bundled)
Social Listening ✅ ⚪
Patient Scribe 🔵 AI-powered patient narrative capture for EGFR mutation testing journey ➖
Bispecifics Pharmacists ➖ (RYBREVANT is bispecific but not in the MM pharmacist context) ➖

Service ERLEADA AKEEGA INLEXZO CARVYKTI

OCE Tracking ⚪ No OCE team identified ➖ ⚪ 🔵 OCE team exists for cell therapy education
MSL / MEE ✅ (GU MSL) ✅ (covered under GU MSL) ✅ (covered under GU MSL) 🎯 HIGH — CAR-T MSLs have unique role in site certification & patient ID
FRM Tracking ✅ ⚪ Covered under GU FRM 🔵 Access is critical for INLEXZO (procedures, scheduling) 🔵 Cell therapy FRM/access is complex
KAM Tracking ✅ ⚪ Covered under Onc KAM 🔵 Account-level dynamics for urology centers 🔵 Treatment center KAM dynamics
ATU ✅ 🔵 Biomarker-driven; ATU on BRCA testing adoption 🎯 HIGH — New launch needs awareness/trial baseline 🎯 HIGH — CAR-T awareness/referral pathway tracking
HCP-Patient Conversations ⚪ ⚪ 🔵 Procedural consent conversations are unique 🔵 Complex shared decision-making for cell therapy
Digital Tracking ⚪ ➖ 🔵 Launch-phase digital promo tracking 🔵 Digital presence for treatment center referral
Patient Experience ⚪ ➖ 🔵 Procedural experience (intravesical delivery) 🎯 HIGH — CAR-T patient journey is long, complex, high unmet need
Qual / Qual Research ⚪ 🔵 Qual on biomarker testing barriers 🔵 Qual on procedural adoption barriers 🔵 Qual on referral pathway barriers
Message Testing 🔵 New data readouts (ATLAS, PROTEUS) need testing 🔵 AMPLITUDE data positioning 🔵 Bladder-preservation message refinement 🔵 Manufacturing/wait time messaging
Commercial Analytics 🔵 SFTO proven for ERLEADA — could expand scope ⚪ ⚪ ⚪
Sales Simulation ⚪ ➖ ⚪ ⚪
Launch KPI Tracking ⚪ Mature brand ⚪ ✅ (likely under Heme Launch KPI umbrella or standalone) 🎯 HIGH — Co-promote launch with Legend needs KPI framework
Ferma Congress ✅ ✅ (bundled) ✅ (bundled under GU) 🔵 Cell therapy congress presence
Social Listening ⚪ ➖ 🔵 Patient communities discussing bladder-sparing options 🔵 CAR-T patient/caregiver online discussion
Patient Scribe ⚪ ➖ ⚪ 🔵 Complex treatment narrative capture
Bispecifics Pharmacists ➖ ➖ ➖ ➖

Section 2: Top 10 White-Space Opportunities (Ranked)

Rank 1: TREMFYA IMM PET Takeover (GI + Derm + Rheum)

Dimension Detail
Brand TREMFYA (Immunology — all sub-TAs)
Service PET / SFEA
Estimated Revenue $250-350K/year (US multi-sub-TA SFE; global adds $200K+ per the TREMFYA proposal pricing)
Why it ranks #1 Already in planning ("2026 IMM PET Takeover" is a documented BD item). This is not a cold pitch — it is a warm handoff from existing KAM/FRM/MSL relationships into the core SFEA product. TREMFYA global SFE alone was priced at $322K.
Entry Strategy Leverage the TREMFYA global proposal momentum. Position IMM PET as the natural next step after KAM, ABS, FRM, and MSL are all running. Frame it as completing the "full field force visibility" picture for Immunology leadership. Reference the DARZALEX/ERLEADA SFEA playbook as proof of enterprise value.
Who to Pitch Jessica Leinert and Liz Beal (IMM Insights leads per Sara Farina's notes); escalate to Brandee Pappalardo (VP, US Medical Affairs, IMM) and Chris Gasink (VP, US Medical Affairs, Gastro) for MSL-adjacent positioning
Timeline Active now — planning document in circulation. Target close by end of Q2 2026.
AO Plan Alignment Directly supports Sara Farina's Non-Onc $1.05M target. This is the single largest opportunity to close the $475K gap.

Rank 2: CARVYKTI PET (Legend Co-Promote)

Dimension Detail
Brand CARVYKTI (ciltacabtagene autoleucel)
Service PET / SFEA
Estimated Revenue $150-200K/year (specialized CAR-T rep tracking, smaller N but complex methodology)
Why it ranks #2 Explicitly called out in Dave's AO plan as a 2H26 milestone ("Legend/J&J co-promote PET for CARVYKTI"). Melissa Rosenberg (AD, Carvykti) and Kazim Ali (Manager, Carvykti) are identified contacts. ZoomRx already provided pricing but never heard back — this needs a direct follow-up push.
Entry Strategy Re-engage Melissa Rosenberg with an updated proposal. Use Kazim Ali (who knows ZoomRx from IMM FRM) as the internal champion. Frame the co-promote PET as essential for measuring Legend vs. J&J rep effectiveness and avoiding finger-pointing when targets are missed.
Who to Pitch Kazim Ali (warm — knows ZoomRx), Melissa Rosenberg (needs re-engagement), escalate to Kyla Metzger (Director, CAR-T)
Timeline 2H26 per AO plan. Proposal refresh should go out by May 2026.
AO Plan Alignment Supports Anna Chubaeva's Onc $4.0M target. New brand entry in Heme.

Rank 3: RYBREVANT Message Testing (MARIPOSA OS + SubQ + HNSCC)

Dimension Detail
Brand RYBREVANT
Service Message Testing
Estimated Revenue $80-120K per round (2-3 rounds likely in FY26-27 across OS data, SubQ, HNSCC) = $160-360K total
Why it ranks #3 Three distinct message testing triggers converging: (1) MARIPOSA OS data maturation, (2) SubQ formulation launch prep (CRL resolved), (3) HNSCC indication expansion. No message testing is currently running for Lung. Cardiopulmonary already runs Message Testing — the capability is proven at J&J.
Entry Strategy Pitch to Mandy Merrill (AD, Lung) and Larry Lee (Director, Lung). Lead with: "You have three major narrative shifts coming in 12 months — OS data, a new formulation, and a new indication. Message testing now ensures reps are armed with tested language before each launch window." Reference the Social Listening finding that patient misconceptions persist about RYBREVANT eligibility — testing can address HCP-facing versions of those misconceptions.
Who to Pitch Mandy Merrill (day-to-day), Larry Lee (Director approval), Dominique Thibault (Product Director, Consumer Activation — triggered Social Listening, may champion)
Timeline Pitch Q2 2026; first round could field by Q3 2026 ahead of data readouts.
AO Plan Alignment Supports Anna's "build awareness/value proposition for new offerings" priority. Opens a new project type in Lung.

Rank 4: INLEXZO PET / SFEA (Bladder Launch Tracking)

Dimension Detail
Brand INLEXZO (TAR-200)
Service PET / SFEA
Estimated Revenue $100-150K/year (smaller N due to specialist audience — urologists, uro-oncologists)
Why it ranks #4 INLEXZO launched September 2025 and reps are actively promoting (ERLEADA URO SOV softened partly because of INLEXZO launch focus pulling share of time). No dedicated SFEA exists for this brand yet. Christina Maloney (AD, Bladder) and Praveen Advani (Director, Bladder) are warm contacts. Praveen's instinct — "if Prostate gets something, we need it too" — is the exact trigger for this sell.
Entry Strategy Lead with the ERLEADA URO Q4 finding: "ERLEADA SOV softened as INLEXZO launch pulled share of time — which means INLEXZO reps are in the field but you have no visibility into their effectiveness." Position INLEXZO PET as the mirror image of the ERLEADA PET that Praveen already values.
Who to Pitch Christina Maloney (day-to-day), Praveen Advani (Director — use "if Prostate gets it, we need it too" framing)
Timeline Pitch immediately. Launch is 6+ months old with no field tracking — urgency framing works.
AO Plan Alignment Directly supports "expanding INLEXZO launch work" noted in Anna's 1H26 milestones.

Rank 5: TREMFYA GI ATU (New Indication Launch)

Dimension Detail
Brand TREMFYA (GI indication)
Service ATU (Awareness, Trial, Usage)
Estimated Revenue $80-120K/year
Why it ranks #5 TREMFYA's GI indication expansion is the biggest IMM growth story. ATU is the standard launch-readiness tracking study and pairs naturally with the SFE work already proposed in the $568K global proposal. GU Oncology and Heme already run ATU — the methodology is proven at J&J.
Entry Strategy Bundle with the TREMFYA global proposal as a complementary workstream: "SFE tells you how your reps are performing. ATU tells you where the market sits on awareness and adoption. Together they give a complete GI launch dashboard."
Who to Pitch Jessica Leinert / Liz Beal (IMM Insights), Lisa Gaynor (per Sara's note to "refocus on Lisa for broader capabilities")
Timeline Pitch alongside TREMFYA global proposal decision. Q2-Q3 2026.
AO Plan Alignment Supports Sara's "expand opportunities (ATU)" priority. Deepens the TREMFYA relationship.

Rank 6: SPRAVATO FRM Tracking (Neuroscience)

Dimension Detail
Brand SPRAVATO
Service FRM Tracking
Estimated Revenue $80-100K/year
Why it ranks #6 SPRAVATO's REMS program creates a uniquely heavy access/reimbursement burden — exactly what FRM tracking is designed to measure. The AO plan explicitly notes "FRM pipeline for potential neuroscience (2H26)." The Q4 2025 SPRAVATO PET data shows LTIP and perception in steep decline driven by Utility Players — FRM tracking would provide the access-side companion data to diagnose whether the problem is rep quality, access barriers, or both.
Entry Strategy Lead with the SPRAVATO Q4 decline: "Your PET shows perception dropping. But you don't know whether that's a message problem or an access problem. FRM tracking separates the two." Position as the diagnostic complement to the PET that is already running. Devon Brown (FRM lead) is warm and open to supplemental work.
Who to Pitch Devon Brown (FRM lead — likes ZoomRx guidance), escalate to Neuro Insights team lead
Timeline 2H26 per AO plan. Pitch by end of Q2 2026.
AO Plan Alignment Directly supports "FRM pipeline for neuroscience" in Dave's AO milestones.

Rank 7: CARVYKTI ATU (CAR-T Referral Pathway Tracking)

Dimension Detail
Brand CARVYKTI
Service ATU
Estimated Revenue $80-120K/year
Why it ranks #7 CAR-T adoption is referral-dependent — community oncologists must identify, refer, and follow patients through treatment center processing. ATU tracks awareness of CARVYKTI vs. Abecma and adoption barriers (manufacturing slots, wait times, eligibility criteria). This data does not exist in any current ZoomRx workstream for Carvykti.
Entry Strategy Bundle with the CARVYKTI PET pitch (Rank #2): "PET measures your reps. ATU measures the market. For a product where the referral funnel is the bottleneck, you need both." Use Kazim Ali's openness to new methods as the entry point.
Who to Pitch Kazim Ali, Melissa Rosenberg, Kyla Metzger
Timeline Pitch alongside CARVYKTI PET in Q2-Q3 2026.
AO Plan Alignment New brand, new service type. Broadens Heme beyond MM-centric work.

Rank 8: RYBREVANT HNSCC Launch KPI Tracking

Dimension Detail
Brand RYBREVANT (Head & Neck indication expansion)
Service Launch KPI Tracking
Estimated Revenue $100-150K (time-bound, 6-12 months)
Why it ranks #8 HNSCC expansion is flagged in the AO plan as a key milestone ("HNSCC expansion for Rybrevant PET"). Launch KPI tracking is the standard ZoomRx offering for new indication launches — proven in Heme (DARZALEX, bispecifics). A new indication means a new HCP audience (head & neck surgeons, radiation oncologists) that the existing Lung PET does not cover.
Entry Strategy Position as a time-limited launch dashboard: "HNSCC is a new specialty for RYBREVANT reps. Launch KPI tracking gives you real-time visibility into awareness, trial, and adoption among a completely different HCP universe." Link to the existing RYBREVANT SFEA data to show how Lung launch metrics evolved.
Who to Pitch Mandy Merrill, Larry Lee, with visibility to Kellie Ward (now leads new product MSLs including Head & Neck)
Timeline Dependent on HNSCC approval timeline. Prep proposal now for rapid deployment.
AO Plan Alignment Directly supports Anna's HNSCC expansion milestone.

Rank 9: TECVAYLI / TALVEY Patient Experience Tracking

Dimension Detail
Brand TECVAYLI + TALVEY
Service Patient Experience Tracking
Estimated Revenue $120-180K/year (cross-bispecific patient experience program)
Why it ranks #9 DARZALEX already runs Patient Experience tracking. TECVAYLI has step-up dosing burden, CRS monitoring, and cost-as-top-challenge (per Q4 PHDM data). TALVEY has unique AEs (skin, nail, taste changes) that no other MM therapy produces. These patient experience stories are materially different from DARZALEX and justify separate tracking. The Bispecifics team (Molly Telford, James Scott) is engaged and data-driven.
Entry Strategy Extend the existing DARZALEX Patient Experience methodology to bispecifics: "DARZALEX patient experience tracking works. But bispecific patients face fundamentally different challenges — step-up dosing, CRS, unique AE profiles. A separate track captures what DARZALEX tracking cannot." James Scott is an advocate who will promote the idea internally.
Who to Pitch James Scott (advocate), Molly Telford, Rick Dunkelberger (Director — wants "what does this mean" framing)
Timeline Q3 2026. Pitch after bispecifics PHDM Q1 2026 data readout provides fresh evidence.
AO Plan Alignment Deepens Heme embed. Opens Patient Experience outside its current Heme-only footprint.

Rank 10: TREMFYA GI Launch KPI Tracking

Dimension Detail
Brand TREMFYA (GI indication)
Service Launch KPI Tracking
Estimated Revenue $80-120K (time-bound)
Why it ranks #10 With the GI indication launch, TREMFYA needs the same launch dashboard that Heme brands have used. This slots naturally alongside the ATU (Rank #5) and the global SFE proposal. Bundling all three creates a comprehensive GI launch intelligence package worth $250-400K.
Entry Strategy Bundle with TREMFYA ATU and global SFE: "Three workstreams, one launch dashboard. SFE for rep effectiveness, ATU for market readiness, Launch KPI for adoption velocity."
Who to Pitch Same as Rank #5 — Jessica Leinert, Liz Beal, Lisa Gaynor
Timeline Aligned with GI indication launch timeline.
AO Plan Alignment Supports Sara's expansion priorities. Adds to the TREMFYA global package revenue.

Revenue Summary: Top 10 Opportunities

Rank Opportunity Est. Revenue Probability Weighted
1 TREMFYA IMM PET Takeover $250-350K 70% $210K
2 CARVYKTI PET (Legend co-promote) $150-200K 50% $88K
3 RYBREVANT Message Testing $160-360K 55% $143K
4 INLEXZO PET / SFEA $100-150K 60% $75K
5 TREMFYA GI ATU $80-120K 55% $55K
6 SPRAVATO FRM $80-100K 50% $45K
7 CARVYKTI ATU $80-120K 40% $40K
8 RYBREVANT HNSCC Launch KPI $100-150K 45% $56K
9 TECVAYLI/TALVEY Patient Exp $120-180K 35% $53K
10 TREMFYA GI Launch KPI $80-120K 45% $45K
TOTAL $1.2-1.85M $810K

If the top 5 close, that is ~$450K in weighted pipeline — nearly enough to cover the entire $475K gap to $5M.

Section 3: Competitive Gap Analysis

Where Competitors Are Entrenched and ZoomRx Is Not

Competitor Strength Area at J&J ZoomRx Vulnerability Defensive Action
ZS Associates CRM-embedded AI copilot (MSL + commercial); integrated into daily workflow; Jesse Heap's AI call plan tool ZoomRx delivers periodic tracking; ZS delivers always-on intelligence embedded in the CRM. If ZS feeds back into call plans using their own data, ZoomRx's SFEA becomes the secondary source. Position SFEA/SFTO as the upstream data that feeds ZS's copilot. Build integration narrative: "ZoomRx generates the primary intelligence; CRM tools operationalize it." Pursue the AI hackathon aggressively (started April 1).
Trinity Life Sciences Analytics and consulting; identified as a competitor in J&J AI landscape per AO plan Trinity competes on strategic consulting — the "so what" layer. If J&J perceives ZoomRx as a data vendor and Trinity as a strategy partner, ZoomRx loses the advisory revenue. Increase consulting tone in deliverables. Cross-workstream themes (SOV-to-impact gap, access gap) are the kind of strategic synthesis that competes with Trinity's value prop. The MEE unification story is a ZoomRx-only asset.
IQVIA Data infrastructure, claims data, real-world evidence; identified as AI competitor per AO plan IQVIA owns the RWE and claims data layer that ZoomRx's SFTO depends on. If IQVIA bundles claims + tracking, ZoomRx is unbundled. IQVIA also has scale advantages in global fielding. Protect the SFTO differentiation by being the integration layer between IQVIA-type data and field effectiveness data. The ERLEADA SFTO and DARZALEX SFTO case studies are proof points. Do not let IQVIA claim the "primary data + claims" bundle.
Sermo / Verilogue HCP panel access, real-time HCP sentiment, chat-based research Sermo's "always-on" panel and quick-turn capabilities could undercut ZoomRx's wave-based model for ad hoc questions. Verilogue's conversation capture could compete with HCP-Patient Conversations. ZoomRx's 57K+ proprietary panel and target list-based recruitment are the moat. Emphasize match rates, on-list sampling, and longitudinal tracking that Sermo cannot do. For HCP-Patient, lead with the INVEGA case study showing insights that self-reported data missed.
BI (Business Intelligence internal) Signal (on Margo platform), EPIC repository, Microsoft Copilot Premium J&J is building internal BI/AI capability. If internal tools can replicate basic tracking metrics (reach, frequency, SOV), the justification for external vendors weakens. Accelerate the AI positioning: Dynamic Dialogues, Patient Scribe, automated reporting. Participate in the AI hackathon to embed ZoomRx into J&J's AI framework, not compete with it. Frame ZoomRx as the external primary data source that internal tools cannot replicate.

Specific Blind Spots by TA

TA Competitive Blind Spot
Lung AstraZeneca's agency partners run their own TAGRISSO field tracking. If J&J benchmarks ZoomRx's RYBREVANT tracking against what they hear about AZ's internal tracking quality, ZoomRx needs to demonstrate superiority on competitive intelligence depth.
GU (Carvykti) Legend Biotech likely has its own tracking vendor for the co-promote. If ZoomRx does not win the J&J-side PET, the co-promote will use Legend's vendor, and ZoomRx loses influence over the methodology.
Immunology AbbVie is the primary competitor for TREMFYA. AbbVie's insights team (supporting Skyrizi/Rinvoq) likely uses a different tracking vendor. If TREMFYA IMM PET does not launch, ZoomRx has no SFEA visibility in the largest immunology competitive battle.
Neuroscience SPRAVATO PET is at risk (steep decline in Q4). If the brand team loses confidence in tracking value while performance is declining, they may cut the tracker rather than invest in complementary FRM. Must frame FRM as the diagnostic tool, not another cost.

Section 4: Connection to Dave Johnson's AO Plan

FY26 Financial Position (as of March 31, 2026)

Metric Value
FY26 Target $5.0M
YTD Bookings $4.1M (82%)
Weighted Pipeline $423K
Gap to Target $475K
Forecast "Expect to achieve or be very close to $5M"

How White-Space Opportunities Map to the Gap

Path 1: Close the gap with 2-3 big wins

Opportunity Weighted Revenue AO Plan Reference
TREMFYA IMM PET Takeover (#1) $210K "Continue MSL expansion; expand FRM/MSL; refocus on Lisa Gaynor"
CARVYKTI PET (#2) $88K "2H26 Legend/J&J co-promote PET for CARVYKTI"
INLEXZO PET (#4) $75K "Expanding INLEXZO launch work"
SPRAVATO FRM (#6) $45K "FRM pipeline for potential neuroscience (2H26)"
Subtotal $418K Closes 88% of the $475K gap

Path 2: Close the gap with volume + strategic wins

Opportunity Weighted Revenue AO Plan Reference
RYBREVANT Message Testing (#3) $143K "Build awareness/value proposition for new offerings"
TREMFYA GI ATU (#5) $55K "Expand opportunities (ATU)"
CARVYKTI ATU (#7) $40K "First ATU win on oncology (MM)" — note: CARVYKTI ATU could be this milestone
RYBREVANT HNSCC Launch KPI (#8) $56K "HNSCC expansion for Rybrevant PET"
TREMFYA GI Launch KPI (#10) $45K "Expand opportunities"
Subtotal $339K Closes 71% of the $475K gap

Recommended approach: Pursue Path 1 as primary (bigger wins, more aligned to existing pipeline) and layer in Path 2 opportunities as supporting deals. If TREMFYA IMM PET and CARVYKTI PET both close, the gap is nearly covered. The remaining $100-150K can be filled with any 1-2 of the medium-sized opportunities.

Alignment to Dave's FY26 Strategic Priorities

AO Priority White-Space Opportunities That Directly Support It
"Seek broader opportunities across J&J (AI, cross-portfolio tracking, HCP-Patient)" TREMFYA IMM PET (#1) is cross-portfolio. CARVYKTI HCP-Patient and RYBREVANT HCP-Patient are new HCP-Patient entry points. AI hackathon participation is the AI lever.
"Elevate partnership at Director/above level" INLEXZO PET pitch goes through Praveen Advani (Director). CARVYKTI PET escalates to Kyla Metzger (Director). TREMFYA IMM PET involves Brandee Pappalardo (VP). Every top-5 opportunity requires Director+ engagement.
"Provide more strategic guidance / consulting" Cross-workstream themes (SOV-to-impact gap, access barrier diagnosis) position ZoomRx as a strategic advisor, not just a data vendor. The SPRAVATO FRM pitch is explicitly framed as a diagnostic tool.
"Gain introduction to Jesse Heap (AI Lead)" AI hackathon (started April 1) is the vehicle. White-space map reinforces why ZoomRx's primary data is essential input to Jesse's AI call plan tool.
"Broaden relationship with Igor Rudychev for cross-brand analytics" Commercial Analytics extensions (ERLEADA SFTO expansion, RYBREVANT SFTO, bispecifics analytics) all feed Igor's data strategy mandate.

By AD Territory

AD Target Key White-Space Plays Combined Weighted Revenue
Anna Chubaeva (Oncology, $4.0M target) CARVYKTI PET, INLEXZO PET, RYBREVANT Message Testing, RYBREVANT HNSCC Launch KPI, TECVAYLI/TALVEY Patient Exp $355K weighted
Sara Farina (Non-Onc, $1.05M target) TREMFYA IMM PET Takeover, TREMFYA GI ATU, TREMFYA GI Launch KPI, SPRAVATO FRM $355K weighted

Balanced across both ADs — neither territory is carrying the burden alone.

Section 5: Quick-Reference Action Calendar

When Action Who Owns Target Contact
April 2026 Re-send CARVYKTI PET proposal to Melissa Rosenberg; loop in Kazim Ali Anna Melissa Rosenberg, Kazim Ali
April 2026 Pitch INLEXZO PET using ERLEADA URO Q4 data as the hook Anna Christina Maloney, Praveen Advani
April-May 2026 Advance TREMFYA IMM PET Takeover planning doc to formal proposal Sara Jessica Leinert, Liz Beal
May 2026 Pitch RYBREVANT Message Testing tied to MARIPOSA OS timeline Anna Mandy Merrill, Larry Lee
May 2026 Pitch SPRAVATO FRM using Q4 LTIP decline as the diagnostic framing Sara/Devon Devon Brown
June 2026 Bundle TREMFYA GI ATU + Launch KPI with global proposal decision Sara Jessica Leinert, Lisa Gaynor
Q3 2026 Pitch CARVYKTI ATU alongside PET (if PET closes) Anna Kazim Ali, Kyla Metzger
Q3 2026 Pitch TECVAYLI/TALVEY Patient Experience after Q1 MEE data readout Anna James Scott, Molly Telford
Q3-Q4 2026 Prep RYBREVANT HNSCC Launch KPI proposal (timing dependent on approval) Anna Mandy Merrill, Kellie Ward
Ongoing Use AI hackathon participation to position ZoomRx as data infrastructure for Jesse Heap's tools Dave Igor Rudychev, Jesse Heap

This map should be refreshed quarterly as projects close, new data emerges, and the competitive landscape shifts. Next scheduled refresh: July 2026.

Section 2: Top 10 White-Space Opportunities (Ranked)
Rank 1: TREMFYA IMM PET Takeover (GI + Derm + Rheum)



Dimension
Detail




Brand
TREMFYA (Immunology — all sub-TAs)


Service
PET / SFEA


Estimated Revenue
$250-350K/year (US multi-sub-TA SFE; global adds $200K+ per the TREMFYA proposal pricing)


Why it ranks #1
Already in planning ("2026 IMM PET Takeover" is a documented BD item). This is not a cold pitch — it is a warm handoff from existing KAM/FRM/MSL relationships into the core SFEA product. TREMFYA global SFE alone was priced at $322K.


Entry Strategy
Leverage the TREMFYA global proposal momentum. Position IMM PET as the natural next step after KAM, ABS, FRM, and MSL are all running. Frame it as completing the "full field force visibility" picture for Immunology leadership. Reference the DARZALEX/ERLEADA SFEA playbook as proof of enterprise value.


Who to Pitch
Jessica Leinert and Liz Beal (IMM Insights leads per Sara Farina's notes); escalate to Brandee Pappalardo (VP, US Medical Affairs, IMM) and Chris Gasink (VP, US Medical Affairs, Gastro) for MSL-adjacent positioning


Timeline
Active now — planning document in circulation. Target close by end of Q2 2026.


AO Plan Alignment
Directly supports Sara Farina's Non-Onc $1.05M target. This is the single largest opportunity to close the $475K gap.




Rank 2: CARVYKTI PET (Legend Co-Promote)



Dimension
Detail




Brand
CARVYKTI (ciltacabtagene autoleucel)


Service
PET / SFEA


Estimated Revenue
$150-200K/year (specialized CAR-T rep tracking, smaller N but complex methodology)


Why it ranks #2
Explicitly called out in Dave's AO plan as a 2H26 milestone ("Legend/J&J co-promote PET for CARVYKTI"). Melissa Rosenberg (AD, Carvykti) and Kazim Ali (Manager, Carvykti) are identified contacts. ZoomRx already provided pricing but never heard back — this needs a direct follow-up push.


Entry Strategy
Re-engage Melissa Rosenberg with an updated proposal. Use Kazim Ali (who knows ZoomRx from IMM FRM) as the internal champion. Frame the co-promote PET as essential for measuring Legend vs. J&J rep effectiveness and avoiding finger-pointing when targets are missed.


Who to Pitch
Kazim Ali (warm — knows ZoomRx), Melissa Rosenberg (needs re-engagement), escalate to Kyla Metzger (Director, CAR-T)


Timeline
2H26 per AO plan. Proposal refresh should go out by May 2026.


AO Plan Alignment
Supports Anna Chubaeva's Onc $4.0M target. New brand entry in Heme.




Rank 3: RYBREVANT Message Testing (MARIPOSA OS + SubQ + HNSCC)



Dimension
Detail




Brand
RYBREVANT


Service
Message Testing


Estimated Revenue
$80-120K per round (2-3 rounds likely in FY26-27 across OS data, SubQ, HNSCC) = $160-360K total


Why it ranks #3
Three distinct message testing triggers converging: (1) MARIPOSA OS data maturation, (2) SubQ formulation launch prep (CRL resolved), (3) HNSCC indication expansion. No message testing is currently running for Lung. Cardiopulmonary already runs Message Testing — the capability is proven at J&J.


Entry Strategy
Pitch to Mandy Merrill (AD, Lung) and Larry Lee (Director, Lung). Lead with: "You have three major narrative shifts coming in 12 months — OS data, a new formulation, and a new indication. Message testing now ensures reps are armed with tested language before each launch window." Reference the Social Listening finding that patient misconceptions persist about RYBREVANT eligibility — testing can address HCP-facing versions of those misconceptions.


Who to Pitch
Mandy Merrill (day-to-day), Larry Lee (Director approval), Dominique Thibault (Product Director, Consumer Activation — triggered Social Listening, may champion)


Timeline
Pitch Q2 2026; first round could field by Q3 2026 ahead of data readouts.


AO Plan Alignment
Supports Anna's "build awareness/value proposition for new offerings" priority. Opens a new project type in Lung.




Rank 4: INLEXZO PET / SFEA (Bladder Launch Tracking)



Dimension
Detail




Brand
INLEXZO (TAR-200)


Service
PET / SFEA


Estimated Revenue
$100-150K/year (smaller N due to specialist audience — urologists, uro-oncologists)


Why it ranks #4
INLEXZO launched September 2025 and reps are actively promoting (ERLEADA URO SOV softened partly because of INLEXZO launch focus pulling share of time). No dedicated SFEA exists for this brand yet. Christina Maloney (AD, Bladder) and Praveen Advani (Director, Bladder) are warm contacts. Praveen's instinct — "if Prostate gets something, we need it too" — is the exact trigger for this sell.


Entry Strategy
Lead with the ERLEADA URO Q4 finding: "ERLEADA SOV softened as INLEXZO launch pulled share of time — which means INLEXZO reps are in the field but you have no visibility into their effectiveness." Position INLEXZO PET as the mirror image of the ERLEADA PET that Praveen already values.


Who to Pitch
Christina Maloney (day-to-day), Praveen Advani (Director — use "if Prostate gets it, we need it too" framing)


Timeline
Pitch immediately. Launch is 6+ months old with no field tracking — urgency framing works.


AO Plan Alignment
Directly supports "expanding INLEXZO launch work" noted in Anna's 1H26 milestones.




Rank 5: TREMFYA GI ATU (New Indication Launch)



Dimension
Detail




Brand
TREMFYA (GI indication)


Service
ATU (Awareness, Trial, Usage)


Estimated Revenue
$80-120K/year


Why it ranks #5
TREMFYA's GI indication expansion is the biggest IMM growth story. ATU is the standard launch-readiness tracking study and pairs naturally with the SFE work already proposed in the $568K global proposal. GU Oncology and Heme already run ATU — the methodology is proven at J&J.


Entry Strategy
Bundle with the TREMFYA global proposal as a complementary workstream: "SFE tells you how your reps are performing. ATU tells you where the market sits on awareness and adoption. Together they give a complete GI launch dashboard."


Who to Pitch
Jessica Leinert / Liz Beal (IMM Insights), Lisa Gaynor (per Sara's note to "refocus on Lisa for broader capabilities")


Timeline
Pitch alongside TREMFYA global proposal decision. Q2-Q3 2026.


AO Plan Alignment
Supports Sara's "expand opportunities (ATU)" priority. Deepens the TREMFYA relationship.




Rank 6: SPRAVATO FRM Tracking (Neuroscience)



Dimension
Detail




Brand
SPRAVATO


Service
FRM Tracking


Estimated Revenue
$80-100K/year


Why it ranks #6
SPRAVATO's REMS program creates a uniquely heavy access/reimbursement burden — exactly what FRM tracking is designed to measure. The AO plan explicitly notes "FRM pipeline for potential neuroscience (2H26)." The Q4 2025 SPRAVATO PET data shows LTIP and perception in steep decline driven by Utility Players — FRM tracking would provide the access-side companion data to diagnose whether the problem is rep quality, access barriers, or both.


Entry Strategy
Lead with the SPRAVATO Q4 decline: "Your PET shows perception dropping. But you don't know whether that's a message problem or an access problem. FRM tracking separates the two." Position as the diagnostic complement to the PET that is already running. Devon Brown (FRM lead) is warm and open to supplemental work.


Who to Pitch
Devon Brown (FRM lead — likes ZoomRx guidance), escalate to Neuro Insights team lead


Timeline
2H26 per AO plan. Pitch by end of Q2 2026.


AO Plan Alignment
Directly supports "FRM pipeline for neuroscience" in Dave's AO milestones.




Rank 7: CARVYKTI ATU (CAR-T Referral Pathway Tracking)



Dimension
Detail




Brand
CARVYKTI


Service
ATU


Estimated Revenue
$80-120K/year


Why it ranks #7
CAR-T adoption is referral-dependent — community oncologists must identify, refer, and follow patients through treatment center processing. ATU tracks awareness of CARVYKTI vs. Abecma and adoption barriers (manufacturing slots, wait times, eligibility criteria). This data does not exist in any current ZoomRx workstream for Carvykti.


Entry Strategy
Bundle with the CARVYKTI PET pitch (Rank #2): "PET measures your reps. ATU measures the market. For a product where the referral funnel is the bottleneck, you need both." Use Kazim Ali's openness to new methods as the entry point.


Who to Pitch
Kazim Ali, Melissa Rosenberg, Kyla Metzger


Timeline
Pitch alongside CARVYKTI PET in Q2-Q3 2026.


AO Plan Alignment
New brand, new service type. Broadens Heme beyond MM-centric work.




Rank 8: RYBREVANT HNSCC Launch KPI Tracking



Dimension
Detail




Brand
RYBREVANT (Head & Neck indication expansion)


Service
Launch KPI Tracking


Estimated Revenue
$100-150K (time-bound, 6-12 months)


Why it ranks #8
HNSCC expansion is flagged in the AO plan as a key milestone ("HNSCC expansion for Rybrevant PET"). Launch KPI tracking is the standard ZoomRx offering for new indication launches — proven in Heme (DARZALEX, bispecifics). A new indication means a new HCP audience (head & neck surgeons, radiation oncologists) that the existing Lung PET does not cover.


Entry Strategy
Position as a time-limited launch dashboard: "HNSCC is a new specialty for RYBREVANT reps. Launch KPI tracking gives you real-time visibility into awareness, trial, and adoption among a completely different HCP universe." Link to the existing RYBREVANT SFEA data to show how Lung launch metrics evolved.


Who to Pitch
Mandy Merrill, Larry Lee, with visibility to Kellie Ward (now leads new product MSLs including Head & Neck)


Timeline
Dependent on HNSCC approval timeline. Prep proposal now for rapid deployment.


AO Plan Alignment
Directly supports Anna's HNSCC expansion milestone.




Rank 9: TECVAYLI / TALVEY Patient Experience Tracking



Dimension
Detail




Brand
TECVAYLI + TALVEY


Service
Patient Experience Tracking


Estimated Revenue
$120-180K/year (cross-bispecific patient experience program)


Why it ranks #9
DARZALEX already runs Patient Experience tracking. TECVAYLI has step-up dosing burden, CRS monitoring, and cost-as-top-challenge (per Q4 PHDM data). TALVEY has unique AEs (skin, nail, taste changes) that no other MM therapy produces. These patient experience stories are materially different from DARZALEX and justify separate tracking. The Bispecifics team (Molly Telford, James Scott) is engaged and data-driven.


Entry Strategy
Extend the existing DARZALEX Patient Experience methodology to bispecifics: "DARZALEX patient experience tracking works. But bispecific patients face fundamentally different challenges — step-up dosing, CRS, unique AE profiles. A separate track captures what DARZALEX tracking cannot." James Scott is an advocate who will promote the idea internally.


Who to Pitch
James Scott (advocate), Molly Telford, Rick Dunkelberger (Director — wants "what does this mean" framing)


Timeline
Q3 2026. Pitch after bispecifics PHDM Q1 2026 data readout provides fresh evidence.


AO Plan Alignment
Deepens Heme embed. Opens Patient Experience outside its current Heme-only footprint.




Rank 10: TREMFYA GI Launch KPI Tracking



Dimension
Detail




Brand
TREMFYA (GI indication)


Service
Launch KPI Tracking


Estimated Revenue
$80-120K (time-bound)


Why it ranks #10
With the GI indication launch, TREMFYA needs the same launch dashboard that Heme brands have used. This slots naturally alongside the ATU (Rank #5) and the global SFE proposal. Bundling all three creates a comprehensive GI launch intelligence package worth $250-400K.


Entry Strategy
Bundle with TREMFYA ATU and global SFE: "Three workstreams, one launch dashboard. SFE for rep effectiveness, ATU for market readiness, Launch KPI for adoption velocity."


Who to Pitch
Same as Rank #5 — Jessica Leinert, Liz Beal, Lisa Gaynor


Timeline
Aligned with GI indication launch timeline.


AO Plan Alignment
Supports Sara's expansion priorities. Adds to the TREMFYA global package revenue.

Section 3: Competitive Gap Analysis
Where Competitors Are Entrenched and ZoomRx Is Not



Competitor
Strength Area at J&J
ZoomRx Vulnerability
Defensive Action




ZS Associates
CRM-embedded AI copilot (MSL + commercial); integrated into daily workflow; Jesse Heap's AI call plan tool
ZoomRx delivers periodic tracking; ZS delivers always-on intelligence embedded in the CRM. If ZS feeds back into call plans using their own data, ZoomRx's SFEA becomes the secondary source.
Position SFEA/SFTO as the upstream data that feeds ZS's copilot. Build integration narrative: "ZoomRx generates the primary intelligence; CRM tools operationalize it." Pursue the AI hackathon aggressively (started April 1).


Trinity Life Sciences
Analytics and consulting; identified as a competitor in J&J AI landscape per AO plan
Trinity competes on strategic consulting — the "so what" layer. If J&J perceives ZoomRx as a data vendor and Trinity as a strategy partner, ZoomRx loses the advisory revenue.
Increase consulting tone in deliverables. Cross-workstream themes (SOV-to-impact gap, access gap) are the kind of strategic synthesis that competes with Trinity's value prop. The MEE unification story is a ZoomRx-only asset.


IQVIA
Data infrastructure, claims data, real-world evidence; identified as AI competitor per AO plan
IQVIA owns the RWE and claims data layer that ZoomRx's SFTO depends on. If IQVIA bundles claims + tracking, ZoomRx is unbundled. IQVIA also has scale advantages in global fielding.
Protect the SFTO differentiation by being the integration layer between IQVIA-type data and field effectiveness data. The ERLEADA SFTO and DARZALEX SFTO case studies are proof points. Do not let IQVIA claim the "primary data + claims" bundle.


Sermo / Verilogue
HCP panel access, real-time HCP sentiment, chat-based research
Sermo's "always-on" panel and quick-turn capabilities could undercut ZoomRx's wave-based model for ad hoc questions. Verilogue's conversation capture could compete with HCP-Patient Conversations.
ZoomRx's 57K+ proprietary panel and target list-based recruitment are the moat. Emphasize match rates, on-list sampling, and longitudinal tracking that Sermo cannot do. For HCP-Patient, lead with the INVEGA case study showing insights that self-reported data missed.


BI (Business Intelligence internal)
Signal (on Margo platform), EPIC repository, Microsoft Copilot Premium
J&J is building internal BI/AI capability. If internal tools can replicate basic tracking metrics (reach, frequency, SOV), the justification for external vendors weakens.
Accelerate the AI positioning: Dynamic Dialogues, Patient Scribe, automated reporting. Participate in the AI hackathon to embed ZoomRx into J&J's AI framework, not compete with it. Frame ZoomRx as the external primary data source that internal tools cannot replicate.



Specific Blind Spots by TA



TA
Competitive Blind Spot




Lung
AstraZeneca's agency partners run their own TAGRISSO field tracking. If J&J benchmarks ZoomRx's RYBREVANT tracking against what they hear about AZ's internal tracking quality, ZoomRx needs to demonstrate superiority on competitive intelligence depth.


GU (Carvykti)
Legend Biotech likely has its own tracking vendor for the co-promote. If ZoomRx does not win the J&J-side PET, the co-promote will use Legend's vendor, and ZoomRx loses influence over the methodology.


Immunology
AbbVie is the primary competitor for TREMFYA. AbbVie's insights team (supporting Skyrizi/Rinvoq) likely uses a different tracking vendor. If TREMFYA IMM PET does not launch, ZoomRx has no SFEA visibility in the largest immunology competitive battle.


Neuroscience
SPRAVATO PET is at risk (steep decline in Q4). If the brand team loses confidence in tracking value while performance is declining, they may cut the tracker rather than invest in complementary FRM. Must frame FRM as the diagnostic tool, not another cost.
